ancestors.notes: Norwegian macrolanguage node; Bokmal is the Dano-Norwegian written standard.
Old Norse (West Norse) — the historical stage underlying all mainland Scandinavian.
Danish superstrate: four centuries of Dano-Norwegian union made written Danish the base of Bokmal; heavy orthographic and lexical layer.

name: Norwegian Bokmål
notes: Norwegian Bokmål — majority written standard (~85%). Derives from Danish writing tradition (Norway-Denmark Union 1380–1814). Oslo/Eastern phonology: alveolar /r/; retroflexion (rn rd rt rs). TONAL ACCENT: Accent 1 and Accent 2 (like Swedish). Source: Kristoffersen (2000). PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode norw1259), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Haugen, E.
sources.title: The Scandinavian Languages: An Introduction to their History

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode norw1259.
